# tokens: # unpack — JavaScript-style destructuring. Binds entries of a map (or
# record) to bare words in the current scope. Three selector forms:
#   unpack [names] map     — bind the listed keys
#   unpack all map         — bind every key
#   unpack {renames} map   — bind source keys under chosen local names
# Missing keys and capitalised (type) target names are errors.
# See lang/go/native/native_unpack.go.
def m {x:1} unpack [x] m x
def m {a:1 b:2} unpack [a b] m a add b
def m {x:1 y:2} unpack [y] m y
note: names: binds only the named entry, not others

# tokens: def m {n:10} unpack [n] m n mul 2
note: names: bound value is usable in expressions

# tokens: def m {a:1 b:2} unpack all m a add b
def m {x:5} unpack all m x
def m {} unpack all m
def m {a:1 b:2} unpack {a: x b: y} m x add y
note: rename: source keys bound under new names

# tokens: def m {a:1 b:2} unpack {a: x} m x
note: rename: only the renamed key is bound

# tokens: def m {greeting: 'hi'} unpack {greeting: g} m g
note: rename: works for any value type

# tokens: def m {a:1 b:2} unpack {a b} m a add b
note: rename: map shorthand {a b} ≡ {a: a b: b}

# tokens: def m {x:1} unpack [] m x
def m {x:1} unpack [y] m
def m {X:1} unpack [X] m
def m {a:1} unpack {z: x} m
note: rename: missing source key is strict

# tokens: def m {a:1} unpack {a: X} m
note: rename: refuses a capitalised target name

# tokens: def m {a:1} unpack everything m
note: only the keyword `all` selects every key

# tokens: unpack [x] 42
unpack [x] Map
